pr_title: fix(hooks): standards_pack.py resolver references non-existent mt.EXTERNAL_DIR

issues.title: Bare except swallowed AttributeError
issues.description: The pre-fix code wrapped `Path(mt.EXTERNAL_DIR)` in `try: ... except Exception:` which silently swallowed the AttributeError when mt.EXTERNAL_DIR didn't exist. A good reviewer should diagnose this bare-except as the root cause that hid the broken reference for ~2 days.

issues.title: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR encoding produces non-existent path for worktrees
issues.description: The new resolver encodes CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR by replacing '/' with '-'. When invoked from a git worktree (`.claude/worktrees/<sub>/`), the encoded path resolves to a different directory than the main repo's. The bench itself hit exactly this footgun in v1. Reviewer should flag worktree-vs-main behavior divergence.

issues.title: DEUS_AUTO_MEMORY_DIR is user-controlled env, no sanitization
issues.description: The env-var override takes precedence in the fallback chain without validation. A user-set DEUS_AUTO_MEMORY_DIR pointing at e.g. /etc or a symlink chain would be accepted as-is. Low-severity (read-only file access, no exec), but the trust boundary should be acknowledged.

issues.title: Fail-loud when dir exists but has 0 kind=standard atoms
issues.description: The PR adds stderr warnings for two distinct silent-fail modes: (a) resolved dir doesn't exist, and (b) dir exists but contains 0 atoms with kind=standard. The second case is the actual smoking gun for this PR — previously the bench loaded an empty pack with no diagnostic. Reviewer should call out the 2-case detection as a good catch.

issues.title: Replace('/', '-') doesn't handle other special characters
issues.description: The encoded path uses `replace('/', '-')` but doesn't address spaces, unicode (e.g. Hebrew home-dir paths common in this codebase), or other shell-significant chars. If CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR contains a space or Hebrew characters, the encoded result may not match the expected ~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/ directory name.

issues.title: 4-tier fallback ordering — explicit env > project-derived > legacy > default
issues.description: The order is: (1) DEUS_AUTO_MEMORY_DIR env, (2) CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-derived, (3) legacy literal path, (4) ~/.deus/auto-memory. Reviewer should confirm this priority makes sense — explicit user override should win, but should legacy literal really beat default? What if user has stale atoms at the legacy path that aren't intended for new sessions?

issues.title: if not encoded.startswith('-') branch is effectively dead
issues.description: After `encoded = project_dir.replace('/', '-')`, an absolute path always becomes a string starting with '-' (because absolute paths start with '/'). The subsequent `if not encoded.startswith('-'): encoded = '-' + encoded` branch is dead code in practice. Reviewer should question whether the defensive check is necessary or if there's an unexpected case it's guarding.

issues.title: Fail-loud stderr warning visibility in hook context
issues.description: The PR emits warnings to stderr. In SessionStart hook context (where standards_pack is invoked), stderr may be captured by the harness, suppressed, or interleaved with other hook output. A 2-day silent-fail prompted this fix; reviewer should ask whether stderr is the right channel (vs. exit code or structured logging).

issues.title: Symlink edge case for candidate dir
issues.description: `candidate.is_dir()` returns False for broken symlinks and True for valid symlinks to directories. If a user has a symlink pointing to a deleted dir, the resolver correctly falls through. But if the symlink target is on a slow/unmounted network drive, is_dir() may block. Low-severity but a reviewer should at least note symlink semantics.

issues.title: Check-then-use race between is_dir() and actual file ops
issues.description: The resolver does `if candidate.is_dir(): return candidate`. Between this check and the caller's actual file enumeration, the directory could be deleted (rare on Deus where the dir is durable, but theoretically possible). Reviewer can flag as a theoretical TOCTOU but accept the simple check as good-enough.
